steps.title: Find the known total
steps.content:
The 9 listed integers have mean $41$, so their total is:
$$9\cdot41=369$$

steps.title: Use the possible mean of all 10 integers
steps.content:
Let $x$ be the missing integer and let $M$ be the mean of all 10 integers. Since $M$ is an integer greater than $41$:
$$\frac{369+x}{10}=M$$
Each integer is positive and less than $60$, so $x\lt 60$. If $M\ge43$, then $x=10M-369\ge61$, which is too large. Therefore $M=42$.

steps.title: Find the missing value
steps.content:
$$\frac{369+x}{10}=42$$
$$369+x=420$$
$$x=51$$
The largest of the original 9 values is $45$, so the largest integer in data set B is 51.
